content: Welcome to the guest-lecture team, Juan. Delighted to have you back with the Environmental Studies students this year.
Thank you, Patricia. Happy to be back. I'll plan the session around land-management policy and the history of environmental regulation.
Great to have you, Juan. The ENV 210 students are looking forward to it. I'll share the class roster and reading list in Slides & Materials.
I've set up the Teams meeting link for the remote session. Juan, we'll do a quick AV check 15 minutes before you go live.
Thanks, Diane. A mic and screen-share check beforehand would be perfect. I'll be presenting from my desktop at home.
The fall guest lecture is confirmed for Thursday, October 22, 2026 at 1:00 PM Eastern, 90 minutes with Q&A at the end.
Meeting link for the October 22 session is live and pinned to this channel. It opens 30 minutes early for setup.
First draft of the slide deck is up: "A Short History of U.S. Environmental Regulation." Feedback welcome before I finalize.
The deck looks excellent, Juan. The Clean Air Act timeline slide will resonate with the class. Maybe add a case study at the end.
Good idea. I'll add a land-management case study drawn from my years at the Department of the Interior and share the updated PDF here.
I've posted the two background readings for students so they come prepared. Slide link and readings are both in this channel.
Mr. Hernandez, how did agencies actually enforce early environmental rules before modern monitoring tools existed?
Great question, Aisha. Early enforcement leaned on field inspections and self-reporting. I'll cover the shift to data-driven monitoring in the lecture.
Will the recording be available afterward? I have a class conflict for part of the session.
Yes, Tyler. We'll record the session and post the link here within a day of the lecture.
Could you recommend a book for students who want to read more on the history of climate policy?
Happy to, Sophia. I'll put together a short reading list and post it in Slides & Materials after the session.
Reminder to the class: our guest lecture with Juan Hernandez is coming up. Please review the posted readings beforehand.
Reminder: I'll open the meeting room 30 minutes early for the AV check. Juan, join whenever you're ready.
Thanks for the reminder. I'll be online early to test the screen share and settle in before students arrive.
The department has confirmed the honorarium arrangement for this term's guest lecture. Thank you again for making time, Juan.
My pleasure, Patricia. These sessions are a highlight of my year. I'll keep the material current for this fall's class.
Final slide deck is posted with the land-management case study added. The file is pinned at the top of this channel.
Perfect timing, Juan. I'll walk the students through the deck in class the week before your session.
Thank you for the earlier answer, Mr. Hernandez. I'm looking forward to the enforcement history part of the lecture.
Quick note: the October 22 session will use the same meeting link pinned here. No new invite needed.
Understood, Diane. I've saved the link and will join from my desktop that afternoon at 12:30 for setup.
Session reminder: the fall guest lecture with Juan is set for October 22 at 1:00 PM. I'll re-post the reading list closer to the date.
Will there be time for open questions at the end, or should we submit them in this channel ahead of time?
Both, Tyler. Post questions here anytime and I'll answer live in the last 20 minutes. That way no one misses out.
Downloaded the slide deck, thank you. The regulation timeline is really clear.
Glad it's helpful, Sophia. Do bring questions to the session; the discussion is always the best part.
Faculty note: Juan Hernandez is confirmed as our environmental-policy guest speaker for the fall term.
Excellent. I'll coordinate the ENV 210 schedule so the lecture lands mid-semester when the policy unit begins.
Booking the October 22 date with Juan and processing his honorarium through the usual college channel.
AV and remote-presenter setup is reserved for the October 22 slot. I'll confirm the room booking with facilities.
ENV 210 class: our guest lecture on environmental policy is scheduled for this term. Details and readings will be shared here.
Looking forward to it. Is the guest speaker joining remotely or in person?
Remotely over Teams, Aisha. We'll project the session in the classroom and open the floor for questions at the end.
